name: Amr ibn al-Hajjaj
title: Guard of the Waterway
shortDescription: The cruel commander of the Kufan army who besieged the Euphrates waterway with 4,000 troops, blocking water from Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan.
fullStory:
Amr ibn al-Hajjaj al-Zubaydi was a tribal leader in Kufa who, like Shabath ibn Rib'i, initially wrote invitation letters to the Imam but joined Ibn Ziyad's court when circumstances changed.

Ibn Ziyad entrusted him with an important mission due to Amr's compliance (his sister Ruwayha was Hani ibn Urwa's wife, and though Ibn Ziyad killed Hani, Amr stayed silent to protect his interests).

Days before Ashura, Amr ibn al-Hajjaj was tasked with 500 (or 4,000) horsemen to besiege the Euphrates waterway and prevent even a drop of water from reaching Imam Hussain's (AS) tents. He brazenly shouted: 'O Hussain! You shall not taste this water until you die of thirst.'

On the day of Ashura, he commanded the right wing of the Kufan army and ordered his soldiers to avoid hand-to-hand combat with the Imam's companions and to just rain arrows on them. He fled after Mukhtar's uprising and died of severe thirst in the desert.
quoteArabic: يا حُسَيْنُ! هذا الفُراتُ تَغَصُّ فِيهِ الكِلابُ... وَاللَّهِ لا تَذُوقُ مِنْهُ قَطْرَةً...
quote: (Addressing Imam Hussain:) O Hussain! This is the Euphrates where dogs wallow... By God, you shall not taste a drop of it until you die of thirst!
